questions.question: What is the primary characteristic of Depth First Search (DFS) algorithm?
options: It explores all the neighbors before moving to the next node
It moves from one node to its most distant neighbor
It explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking
It only visits each node once
questions.explanation: DFS algorithm explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

questions.question: Which data structure is typically used to implement Depth First Search?
options: Stack
Queue
Tree
Graph
questions.explanation: DFS algorithm can be implemented using a stack data structure.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of Depth First Search over Breadth First Search?
options: It explores more nodes
It is faster
It is more memory-efficient
It is better for unweighted graphs
questions.explanation: DFS algorithm is faster than BFS algorithm, especially for unweighted graphs.

questions.question: In a Depth First Search traversal, what happens when a dead end is reached?
options: The traversal continues to the next node
The traversal goes back to the previous node
The traversal stops and Backtracks
The traversal restarts from the beginning
questions.explanation: When a dead end is reached in a DFS traversal, the traversal stops and backtracks to the previous node.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a problem that can be solved using Depth First Search?
options: Finding the shortest path in a graph
Finding the minimum spanning tree of a graph
Finding all the connected components of a graph
Finding a Hamiltonian cycle in a graph
questions.explanation: DFS algorithm can be used to find all the connected components of a graph.
